1.name: Now
1.hand: Both hands form bent or Y-shapes with palms facing upward, then drop downward sharply and simultaneously in a single decisive motion. The movement is quick and definitive, stopping abruptly at the bottom.
1.face: Neutral to assertive expression. Eyebrows relaxed. No grammatical brow movement required for a statement. Eye contact maintained with the camera.
1.body: Upright torso. Both arms drop together at chest to waist height. The motion is sharp and coordinated. Shoulders relaxed.

2.name: Later
2.hand: Dominant hand forms an L-shape — index finger extended upward and thumb extended outward at roughly 90 degrees. Palm faces to the side. The L-handshape rotates forward and downward from a vertical position to a horizontal one in a smooth arc.
2.face: Neutral expression. Eyebrows relaxed. No grammatical brow movement required. Eye contact maintained.
2.body: Upright torso. Dominant forearm and wrist produce the rotation. Hand held at chest to shoulder height. Movement is smooth and controlled.

3.name: Today
3.hand: Both hands form Y-shapes or bent hands with palms facing up. Both hands drop downward sharply (NOW), then immediately repeat the same downward drop motion a second time. Two distinct drops in quick succession.
3.face: Neutral, clear expression. Eyebrows relaxed. No grammatical brow movement required for a declarative statement. Eye contact maintained.
3.body: Upright torso. Both hands drop at chest to waist level twice in clean succession. Movement is deliberate and symmetrical.

4.name: Go
4.hand: Both index fingers extended and pointing upward or forward. Both hands arc outward simultaneously away from the body in the intended direction of travel — a smooth forward-and-outward arc ending with index fingers pointing outward. Alternatively, one hand arcs forward.
4.face: Neutral to slightly purposeful expression. Eyebrows relaxed. No grammatical brow movement required. Eye contact maintained.
4.body: Upright posture. Arms extend forward and outward during the arc. Movement originates from the elbows and wrists. Shoulders relaxed and open.

5.name: Play
5.hand: Both hands form Y-shapes — thumb and pinky extended, middle three fingers curled in. Both wrists rotate or wiggle alternately or simultaneously in a loose, repeated back-and-forth motion at chest level.
5.face: Light, playful, or neutral expression. Slight smile acceptable. Eyebrows relaxed. Expression may convey enjoyment or leisure.
5.body: Upright, relaxed posture. Both hands held at chest height with wrists rotating freely. The motion is loose and fluid, not stiff. Shoulders relaxed.

6.name: Visit
6.hand: Both hands form V-shapes — index and middle fingers extended and spread. Both V-hands rotate in alternating forward circles at chest level, one moving forward while the other moves back, in a continuous reciprocating motion.
6.face: Warm, friendly, or neutral expression. Slight smile acceptable. Eyebrows relaxed. Expression may convey social warmth.
6.body: Upright posture. Both hands held at mid-chest height rotating in alternating circles. Movement is smooth and continuous. Shoulders relaxed and open.

7.name: Listen
7.hand: Dominant hand forms a cupped or L-shape and is brought to the ear or the side of the face near the ear. The hand may cup behind the ear or rest near it. Movement is deliberate — hand moves toward the ear area clearly.
7.face: Attentive, focused expression. Head may tilt slightly toward the signing hand as if straining to hear. Eyebrows may rise slightly to convey attentiveness.
7.body: Upright torso with a possible slight head tilt. Dominant arm raises so the hand reaches ear level. Shoulders relaxed. Body posture should convey attentiveness.

8.name: Draw
8.hand: Non-dominant hand is held open and flat with palm facing upward at chest level. Dominant hand forms an I-shape — pinky extended, other fingers curled — and traces a wavy or zigzag line downward across the non-dominant palm, mimicking a pen drawing on paper.
8.face: Neutral, focused expression. Eyebrows relaxed. No grammatical brow movement required. Eye contact maintained.
8.body: Upright torso. Non-dominant hand held flat as a surface. Dominant hand traces across it with a clear wavy motion. Both hands are visible and at natural chest height.

9.name: Swim
9.hand: Both hands held flat with fingers together and palms facing downward. Both hands make simultaneous or alternating forward breaststroke-like movements — sweeping outward and forward from the chest in smooth arcing motions that mimic swimming strokes.
9.face: Neutral to slightly active expression. Eyebrows relaxed. No grammatical brow movement required. Eye contact maintained with camera.
9.body: Upright torso. Both arms move forward from the chest in breaststroke arcs. Movement is fluid and symmetrical. Shoulders involved naturally in the forward sweeping motion.

10.name: Finish
10.hand: Both hands open with fingers spread wide, palms initially facing inward toward the body. Both hands then flip outward simultaneously and sharply so palms now face away from the body. The flip is sudden and decisive — a sharp outward rotation of both wrists at the same time.
10.face: Decisive, conclusive expression. Eyebrows may raise slightly to emphasize the finality. Firm, clear expression. Eye contact maintained.
10.body: Upright torso. Both hands held at chest level before the flip. The outward rotation is abrupt and visible. Shoulders relaxed. The sharp bilateral motion should be clearly readable as a definitive end.
